sleep: Try keeping your bedtime within 30 minutes of the same time every night — consistency matters more than duration.
Avoid screens 30–60 min before bed. Blue light suppresses melatonin by up to 50%.
A cool room (65–68°F) significantly improves deep sleep quality.
bench: If bench is stalling, try a 10% deload for one week then re-test. Fatigue masks fitness.
Add 1 set of incline close-grip press to bring up tricep lockout strength.
Wrist position matters — keep them stacked directly over elbows to reduce joint stress.
squat: Box squats 2 inches below parallel can help reinforce depth without loading the spine.
Goblet squats as warm-up sets groove the pattern and open hip flexors pre-lift.
deadlift: Reset your hips — if your hips shoot up first, your lower back is doing the work.
Hip hinge drills with a dowel rod on your back can fix bar path issues fast.
motivation: On low motivation days, commit to just the warm-up. You almost always finish the session once you start.
Track the win streaks, not just the PRs — consistency is the meta-skill.
nutrition: Protein synthesis peaks at ~40g per meal. Spreading protein across 4 meals beats front-loading it.
Pre-workout carbs 90 min before a session improve output more than caffeine alone.
recovery: Two consecutive hard sessions on the same muscle group without 48hrs rest blunts adaptation.
Heart rate variability (HRV) is a better readiness signal than how sore you feel.
sore: DOMS peaks 24–48hrs after training. Light movement (walk, stretch) clears it faster than rest.
Contrast showers (hot/cold cycling) can reduce next-day soreness by ~20%.
